concerns.text: FR-005 and SC-001 require correlation between manual continuity scores and divergence metrics. Task T023 implements correlation between 'Internal Divergence and External Optical Flow Variance', completely missing the required manual score correlation. No task exists to correlate manual scores with divergence.

SC-001 mandates outputting Pearson r and Spearman rho against the manual ground truth. T023 calculates these against optical flow variance. The spec's primary hypothesis (manual score vs divergence) is unimplemented.

FR-006 and SC-003 require sensitivity analysis on classification thresholds using the manual scores (binarized). T024 mentions 'sweep thresholds' but the context of T023 (optical flow) and the plan's 'Binarized labels' section implies the sensitivity is on the optical flow proxy, not the manual score vs divergence relationship defined in FR-006.

SC-004 requires recording the distribution of manual scores and calculating variance. T021 implements this, but the task description says 'calculate variance and histogram of manual scores; explicitly report variance (SC-004)'. This is covered, but the subsequent correlation tasks (T023) ignore these manual scores, breaking the chain of evidence for SC-001.

Task T023 description: 'compute Pearson r and Spearman rho between Internal Divergence and External Optical Flow Variance'. This contradicts the spec's FR-005 which mandates correlation between 'manual continuity scores and the computed divergence metrics'. The task implements the Plan's alternative approach, not the Spec's requirement.
